sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after clicking the yellow gap. The animation changed 2 cells in bbox [0, 61]-[0, 62], which is still the pink timer bar area. Let me check if any gameplay objects changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking the yellow gap
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

# Build comparison by color and bbox instead of id
def get_objects(seg):
    objs = []
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        objs.append({
            'color': n['color'],
            'pixels': n['pixels'],
            'hash': n['hash'],
            'bbox': (min(rows), min(cols), max(rows), max(cols))
        })
    return objs

before_objs = get_objects(before)
after_objs = get_objects(after)

print("Before:")
for o in before_objs:
    print(f"  color={o['color']} pixels={o['pixels']} bbox={o['bbox']}")
print("\nAfter:")
for o in after_objs:
    print(f"  color={o['color']} pixels={o['pixels']} bbox={o['bbox']}")

# Check pixel counts by color
from collections import Counter
before_counts = Counter(o['color'] for o in before_objs)
after_counts = Counter(o['color'] for o in after_objs)
print(f"\nBefore counts: {dict(before_counts)}")
print(f"After counts: {dict(after_counts)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
